meta.title: NIPAH ROUTE MAP
meta.source: The Meghalayan Express / Health Minister Muraleedharan — all VHR and HR contacts complete 21-day quarantine (major milestone); only 3 LR contacts remain; patient on ventilator, last 2 samples negative; 58 Nipah-like samples negative since Jun 10. Jun 30 2026
meta.disclaimer: Map coordinates are indicative landmarks for public awareness. Refer to official Health Department bulletins for the authoritative route map.

patient.status: Critical — on ventilator support, isolation ICU; last two samples (week of Jun 24–30) tested negative for Nipah; medical board to decide further treatment. Second dose of monoclonal antibody therapy (m102.4) administered per ICMR guidelines (first dose Jun 13); Remdesivir and Ribavirin ongoing

contacts.note: 104 total contacts (4 very-high-risk, 14 high-risk, 86 low-risk initially). As of Jun 24: 11 low-risk contacts discharged after 21-day observation. As of Jun 27: 4 more contacts discharged — 3 high-risk and 1 low-risk; 89 contacts monitored (4 VHR, 11 HR, 74 LR). As of Jun 30: ALL persons in very-high-risk and high-risk categories have completed 21-day quarantine without symptoms — milestone declared by Health Minister Muraleedharan. On Jun 30, a further 3 HR and 24 LR contacts were released; only 3 low-risk contacts remain under surveillance. All 9 contacts previously admitted to hospital have been discharged. All 15 persons who developed symptoms while on contact list tested negative. 58 samples from persons with Nipah-like symptoms in Kozhikode district since Jun 10 have tested negative. No new Nipah cases confirmed. Patient remains on ventilator at Kozhikode Medical College; last two samples tested negative for Nipah. Bat samples from roosting sites near patient's residence tested negative (Jun 22); source of infection remains unidentified.
